ogTitle: Piccadilly vs Pike River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Piccadilly and Pike River.
heading: Piccadilly vs Pike River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Piccadilly, South Australia and Pike River, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Piccadilly has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Pike River (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Piccadilly or Pike River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Piccadilly has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Pike River scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Piccadilly scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Pike River scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Piccadilly or Pike River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Piccadilly has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
